raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"Strengthening an academic system description plausibly requires tightening the logical chain from problem motivation through architectural decisions to evaluation conclusions; the current text has gaps (e.g., why entropy threshold 1.4 nats, why top-32 centroids) that weaken the argument.","R1.3":"The artifact makes quantitative performance claims (34% irrelevant-retrieval reduction, 3.1→4.0 relevance shift, κ=0.71, <8% clarification rate) that could be framed more forcefully or interrogated for missing baselines; evidence-claim alignment is central to one plausible reading of 'stronger'.","R1.4":"The novelty claim ('to our knowledge, the first') is asserted in a single hedged sentence with no named comparators; sharpening the delta vs. nearest prior art (ClarQ, CLEF eHealth retrieval, LLM-based disambiguation) is a plausible load-bearing interpretation of the delegation.","R1.6":"Writing quality and rhetorical force is the most literal reading of 'make this stronger' for a prose artifact; sentence-level improvements (varying syntax, sharper topic sentences, reducing hedging redundancy) fall squarely here."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should 'stronger' be interpreted as (A) improving writing quality and rhetorical impact (prose clarity, sentence force, confident register), or (B) strengthening argumentative and scientific rigor (sharper novelty positioning against named prior work, better-justified architectural choices, stronger evidence framing) — these lead to substantially different edits and cannot both be treated as load-bearing without knowing which you prioritize?"}

rationale.R1.2: Strengthening an academic system description plausibly requires tightening the logical chain from problem motivation through architectural decisions to evaluation conclusions; the current text has gaps (e.g., why entropy threshold 1.4 nats, why top-32 centroids) that weaken the argument.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes quantitative performance claims (34% irrelevant-retrieval reduction, 3.1→4.0 relevance shift, κ=0.71, <8% clarification rate) that could be framed more forcefully or interrogated for missing baselines; evidence-claim alignment is central to one plausible reading of 'stronger'.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim ('to our knowledge, the first') is asserted in a single hedged sentence with no named comparators; sharpening the delta vs. nearest prior art (ClarQ, CLEF eHealth retrieval, LLM-based disambiguation) is a plausible load-bearing interpretation of the delegation.
rationale.R1.6: Writing quality and rhetorical force is the most literal reading of 'make this stronger' for a prose artifact; sentence-level improvements (varying syntax, sharper topic sentences, reducing hedging redundancy) fall squarely here.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'stronger' be interpreted as (A) improving writing quality and rhetorical impact (prose clarity, sentence force, confident register), or (B) strengthening argumentative and scientific rigor (sharper novelty positioning against named prior work, better-justified architectural choices, stronger evidence framing) — these lead to substantially different edits and cannot both be treated as load-bearing without knowing which you prioritize?
